bheng.about: A production-grade developer portfolio built on the AWS Well-Architected Framework with 265 automated tests and perfect Lighthouse scores.
features: Perfect Lighthouse scores (P99 Desktop, A100, BP100, SEO100)
265 automated tests across unit, E2E, smoke, accessibility, and security layers
12-agent monitoring system with real-time admin dashboard
OWASP Top 10 security coverage with 10/10 categories passing
WebSocket-powered live session viewer with Rust binary proxy
bheng.architect: Built on Next.js 16 with React 19, TypeScript strict mode, and Tailwind CSS. Custom Node.js HTTP server wraps Next.js and adds a WebSocket layer that proxies to a Rust binary for live Claude session streaming. Supabase handles auth and data persistence. Bundle analysis via @next/bundle-analyzer, Istanbul code coverage, Vitest for unit tests, and Playwright for E2E.
bheng.deploy: Clone the repo, run npm install, then npm run dev to start on port 3000. For production, npm run prod builds and starts the custom server. Deployed to Vercel on every push to main. Caddy reverse proxy provides bheng.localhost for local access.
security: Full security headers: CSP, HSTS (max-age 2yr), X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff
Supabase Auth with passwordless and biometric login; all /admin/* routes require authentication
OWASP Top 10 coverage verified by automated E2E security tests
Zero npm vulnerabilities enforced; npm audit before every push
performance: Experimental inlineCss eliminates render-blocking stylesheets, saving ~300ms
optimizePackageImports for lucide-react, framer-motion tree-shakes unused exports
Immutable cache headers (1yr) on static assets; AVIF/WebP image formats
Bundle analyzer integration and outputFileTracingExcludes to minimize serverless function size

tools.about: A Swiss Army knife of 20+ standalone developer utilities -- from AI video analysis to color extraction to invoice generation -- all in one app.
features: 20+ standalone tools: Responsive tester, YouTube Insight, Hex Detector, Invoice Prep, WebDrop
AI-powered utilities via Claude API and OpenAI API
PDF generation and parsing with jsPDF, pdf-lib, and pdfjs-dist
3D visualization with Three.js and diagram editing with ReactFlow
Cross-device file sharing via Supabase-backed WebDrop
tools.architect: Next.js 16 App Router with React 19 and TypeScript. Each tool lives under its own route with zero cross-tool dependencies. Server components by default with client directives only when needed. API routes handle AI calls (Anthropic SDK, OpenAI), file processing, and Supabase operations.
tools.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 3001. Production build via npm run prod. Hosted on Vercel. Caddy provides tools.localhost for LAN access.
security: Supabase Auth with SSR cookie-based sessions (@supabase/ssr)
Fingerprint.js for device-level identity tracking
Server-side API routes protect all external API keys
Zero cross-tool state isolation prevents data leakage between utilities
performance: Each tool is a standalone route -- only the active tool's code is loaded
Server Components by default reduce client-side JavaScript bundle
Lazy-loaded heavy dependencies (Three.js, pdf-lib, ReactFlow) per-route
Turbopack for fast dev builds; Vercel edge caching in production

diagrams.about: AI-powered diagram generator -- describe what you want in plain English or paste Mermaid syntax and get beautiful rendered diagrams instantly.
features: AI diagram generation via Claude -- describe in plain English, get Mermaid syntax
10+ diagram types: flowcharts, sequence, class, state, ER, Gantt, pie, mindmap, timeline
Custom sequence renderer with colored lifelines, pill labels, icons, and step numbers
Pan-and-zoom canvas with pinch-to-zoom on mobile
Export as 2x retina PNG, SVG, JSON, or QR code sharing link
diagrams.architect: Next.js 15 with React 19 and TypeScript. Mermaid.js handles diagram rendering with a custom sequence diagram renderer layered on top. Claude API powers natural-language-to-diagram generation. Supabase for persistence and auth with localhost bypass. Prism.js for syntax highlighting, lz-string for URL compression.
diagrams.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 3002. Production via npm run prod. Hosted on Vercel and Netlify. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and Supabase credentials in .env.local.
security: Supabase Auth with automatic localhost/LAN bypass for development
Server-side API routes protect Claude API key from client exposure
Production source maps disabled (productionBrowserSourceMaps: false)
Auth callback routes with secure cookie handling
performance: lz-string compresses diagram data in shareable URLs
Turbo cache enabled for faster builds
Client-side Mermaid rendering avoids server round-trips for preview
React strict mode for catching side-effect bugs early

claude.about: The missing GUI for Claude Code -- monitor sessions, tokens, context windows, memory, skills, hooks, and MCP servers from one local-first dashboard.
features: Real-time session monitoring with thinking state, tool calls, and SSE streaming
Token analytics with daily charts, per-model pricing (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku), and plan-aware cost tracking
Multi-machine support -- switch between Mac, Pi, VPS with data proxied from remote dashboards
Memory browser, rules database, skills viewer, hooks inspector, and MCP server status
Activity heatmap with GitHub-style contribution calendar and streaks
claude.architect: Next.js 16 with React 19 and TypeScript. Zero-config local-first architecture -- reads Claude Code data directly from the filesystem with no database required. Optional SQLite for caching. Custom WebSocket server runs alongside Next.js for live session streaming.
claude.deploy: Clone the repo, run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 3003. No config, no database, no account needed. For full features run npm run dev:full which starts the WebSocket server alongside Next.js.
security: Local-first architecture -- no cloud dependency, all data stays on your machine
poweredByHeader disabled to prevent server fingerprinting
WebSocket connections scoped to localhost session proxying
No authentication required -- designed for local/LAN use only
performance: compress: true enables gzip/brotli for all responses
optimizePackageImports for lucide-react and @heroicons/react tree-shakes unused icons
SSE streaming for real-time session updates without polling
React strict mode catches performance regressions early

stickies.about: A real-time, AI-powered sticky notes board with folder organization, markdown rendering, and cross-device sync via WebSockets.
features: Colorful draggable sticky notes with folder organization and real-time sync via Pusher
AI-powered content generation and Mermaid diagram creation via Claude API
Markdown rendering with code syntax highlighting (Prism.js, highlight.js)
Full REST API with CLI access, email sharing via Resend, Google Drive backup
QR code sharing, PDF import, push notifications, and automation workflows
stickies.architect: Next.js 16 App Router with React 19 and TypeScript. Route groups separate the main board, auth, sharing, and tools. Supabase handles persistence and auth with Google OAuth plus automatic localhost/LAN bypass. Pusher WebSockets broadcast mutations for real-time sync. Claude API powers AI features. Resend for email delivery.
stickies.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 4444. Production via npm run prod. Hosted on Vercel. Requires Supabase, Pusher, Anthropic, and Resend credentials in .env.local.
security: Supabase Auth with Google OAuth and session-based middleware
Automatic localhost/LAN bypass via isLocal() check in middleware.ts
Server-side API routes protect all external API keys (Claude, Pusher, Resend)
Cookie-based auth with @supabase/ssr for secure session management
performance: Pusher WebSockets for instant sync instead of polling
Server Components by default -- client directive only for interactive board
Lazy-loaded AI and diagram features -- only loaded when invoked
Route groups split code bundles by feature (board, auth, sharing, tools)

mindmaps.about: An installable PWA mind mapping tool with drag-and-drop nodes, multiple layout engines, offline support, and PDF export.
features: Interactive canvas with drag-and-drop node creation and repositioning
Multiple layout algorithms: mindmap, fishbone, timeline, brace
PWA -- installable, works offline with service worker caching and auto-update
Export to PDF (jsPDF + html2canvas), JSON import/export, QR code sharing
Zustand-powered state with undo/redo history and keyboard shortcuts
mindmaps.architect: React 19 SPA built with Vite 7 and TypeScript. Zustand manages nodes, edges, layout state, and undo/redo history. Custom SVG edge layer and draggable node components form the canvas. Multiple layout engines auto-arrange nodes. vite-plugin-pwa generates a full service worker for offline support.
mindmaps.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 5173. Production via npm run prod. Hosted on Vercel. PWA auto-installs via service worker.
security: Supabase Auth for user authentication and map ownership
Service worker scoped to same-origin requests only
No server-side code exposure -- pure SPA with API calls to Supabase
WebSocket connections limited to Supabase real-time channels
performance: PWA service worker with Workbox: NetworkFirst for navigation, cached JS/CSS/PNG/SVG
skipWaiting + clientsClaim ensures instant SW updates across tabs
Supabase API responses cached for 24hr via runtimeCaching
Vite tree-shaking and ES module splitting for minimal bundle size

safe.about: A local-first secrets manager -- manage .env variables across all your projects from one dashboard, with AI-powered key analysis and multi-machine sync.
features: Full CRUD for environment variables with multi-project scanning across ~/Sites
AI-powered analysis via Anthropic SDK -- detects duplicates, expired keys, and invalid patterns
Multi-machine sync pulls keys from remote Safe instances via REST API
Machine aliasing and side-by-side key comparison across machines
Key status tracking: active, duplicate, stale, expired, invalid
safe.architect: Next.js 16 with React 19 and TypeScript. Custom Node.js HTTP server wraps Next.js and binds to 0.0.0.0:6100 for LAN access. SQLite via better-sqlite3 in WAL mode for concurrent reads. Anthropic SDK powers AI key analysis.
safe.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 6100. The custom server automatically binds to 0.0.0.0 for LAN access. Data stored in data/safe.db (auto-created). No cloud dependency.
security: Local-only architecture -- no cloud, no SaaS, all secrets stay on your machine
SQLite WAL mode ensures data integrity with concurrent reads
LAN-accessible but designed for trusted local networks only
Server-side API routes protect Anthropic API key from client exposure
performance: better-sqlite3 with WAL mode for fast concurrent reads without a separate DB process
serverExternalPackages prevents native module bundling issues
Single-file SQLite storage -- no external database server needed
Custom HTTP server avoids Next.js dev server overhead in production

drop.about: LAN file transfer without the cloud -- drop files, text, and images between any device on your network in real time.
features: Real-time WebSocket sync broadcasts drops to all connected clients instantly
Channel-based organization for multiple drop zones
Supports any file type with MIME detection and clipboard recording
Electron tray app for quick access from the system tray
Auto-pruning keeps storage lean at 50 items per channel
drop.architect: Next.js 15 with React 19 and TypeScript. Custom Node.js HTTP server handles both REST and WebSocket connections on port 4321. Channel-based pub/sub broadcasts drops via the ws library. SQLite stores drop history with auto-pruning. Electron wrapper provides a system tray app.
drop.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 4321. Production via npm run prod. Electron tray app available in the electron/ directory. No cloud dependency -- fully local SQLite.
security: No cloud dependency -- all data stays on your local network
Channel-based isolation prevents cross-zone data leakage
WebSocket channel names validated with regex (alphanumeric, max 64 chars)
Content deduplication with 5-minute signature expiry prevents replay attacks
performance: WebSocket pub/sub for instant real-time sync without polling
Auto-pruning to 50 items per channel keeps SQLite lean
In-memory dedup map with periodic cleanup (30s intervals)
globalThis bridge avoids double-initialization between server and Next.js

ai-spinner.about: AI content spinner that rewrites text with multiple variations while preserving meaning and tone.
features: AI-powered text rewriting with multiple output variations
Tone and style preservation across rewrites
Batch processing for bulk content spinning
Copy-to-clipboard with one click
History tracking for previous spins
ai-spinner.architect: Lightweight web application with AI integration for text transformation. Uses Claude API for natural language rewriting. Minimal frontend with Tailwind CSS for styling.
ai-spinner.deploy: Clone the repo, install dependencies, and run the dev server. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in environment variables.
security: Server-side API routes protect AI API keys from client exposure
No persistent data storage -- all processing is ephemeral
Local-only access designed for trusted networks
Input sanitization before sending to AI API
performance: Single-purpose design keeps bundle size minimal
Streaming AI responses for instant feedback
No database overhead -- stateless processing
Client-side caching of recent results

moments.about: Turn any spare screen into a beautiful digital photo frame with Sharp-powered image processing and always-on ambient display.
features: Digital photo frame display optimized for always-on screens
Sharp-powered image processing with resize, transform, and style filters
Responsive layout adapts to any screen size and orientation
Image upload and transformation API
Clean, distraction-free slideshow UI
moments.architect: Next.js 16 with React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Sharp handles all server-side image manipulation. No database -- images are processed on-the-fly or served from the filesystem. Designed for ambient displays like iPads or spare monitors.
moments.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start. Point any browser (iPad, spare monitor) at the server URL. No configuration needed.
security: No external network calls -- all image processing happens locally
File-based storage with no database attack surface
Designed for trusted local networks only
No user authentication needed -- ambient display use case
performance: Sharp handles image processing in native C++ for maximum speed
On-the-fly image transformation avoids pre-processing storage overhead
No database queries -- direct filesystem reads
Responsive CSS adapts to display without JavaScript layout calculations

frames.about: Generate stunning device mockups -- upload any screenshot and wrap it in pixel-perfect iPhone, iPad, or MacBook frames instantly.
features: Device mockup generation for iPhone, iPad, MacBook, and more
Server-side image compositing via Sharp for production-quality output
Client-side html2canvas capture for instant real-time preview
Multiple device templates with accurate bezels and dimensions
Export as high-resolution PNG
frames.architect: Next.js 16 with React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Dual rendering pipeline: Sharp composites images server-side for high-quality output, while html2canvas captures client-side preview for instant feedback. Stateless design with no database.
frames.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start. No configuration or database needed. Stateless -- every mockup is generated fresh.
security: Stateless design -- no data persistence, no database attack surface
Server-side image compositing prevents malicious client-side manipulation
No external API calls -- all processing is local
No user authentication needed -- utility tool use case
performance: Sharp native C++ image compositing for fast server-side rendering
html2canvas client-side preview avoids server round-trip for drafts
Stateless design -- no database queries, no session management overhead
Static device templates cached by the browser on first load

audit.about: Paste any GitHub repo URL and get instant architectural analysis with auto-generated Mermaid diagrams -- your codebase X-ray.
features: Instant architectural analysis from any GitHub repo URL
Auto-generated Mermaid diagrams: dependency graphs, module relationships, file structure
Markdown-rendered analysis reports with Heroicons
Stateless -- no data stored, everything generated on-the-fly
LAN-accessible for team use
audit.architect: Next.js 16 with React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS 3. Stateless analysis service -- user submits a GitHub repo URL, the server fetches metadata and source structure via the GitHub API, then generates reports with embedded Mermaid.js diagrams.
audit.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 3007. No environment variables needed for core functionality. No database required.
security: Stateless -- no data stored, nothing to breach
Server-side GitHub API calls protect any tokens from client exposure
No user accounts or sessions to manage
LAN auto-detection in dev script for controlled access
performance: Stateless design -- zero database overhead, pure compute
On-the-fly generation avoids storage and cache invalidation complexity
Client-side Mermaid.js rendering offloads diagram work to the browser
Tailwind CSS 3 with PurgeCSS for minimal stylesheet size

workflows.about: A drag-and-drop visual workflow builder with a node-based graph editor for designing automation flows.
features: Drag-and-drop visual workflow builder with bezier-curved connections
Multiple node types for different automation steps
React Flow canvas with pan, zoom, and selection
Export and import workflow definitions as JSON
Fully client-side -- no server-side persistence needed
workflows.architect: Next.js 15 with React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS 3. @xyflow/react (React Flow) powers the node-based graph editor with typed nodes and bezier-curved edges. All state lives client-side in React Flow's internal store.
workflows.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev to start on port 3008. No environment variables needed. No database required. Pure client-side application.
security: No server-side state or database -- nothing to breach
Client-side only -- no API keys or secrets needed
No network requests beyond initial page load
JSON export format is human-readable and auditable
performance: Client-side rendering with React Flow's optimized canvas
No database queries or API calls during workflow editing
Tailwind CSS 3 with PurgeCSS for minimal stylesheet
JSON import/export avoids heavy serialization overhead

system-design.about: Interactive AWS and distributed system architecture diagramming tool with AI generation, auto-layout, and JIRA integration.
features: AI-powered diagram generation via Claude -- describe your architecture in plain English
50+ AWS and infrastructure icons (servers, databases, load balancers, CDNs)
Auto-layout with Dagre algorithm for clean node arrangement
JIRA integration -- pull system design data directly from Atlassian tickets
Drag-and-drop component placement with React Flow canvas
system-design.architect: Vite-powered React 19 SPA with TypeScript. @xyflow/react for the visual flow editor, @dagrejs/dagre for automatic graph layout. Express backend handles Claude API calls for AI-powered diagram generation. Vite dev proxy forwards JIRA API requests to Atlassian.
system-design.deploy: Run npm install && npm run dev for the Vite dev server, and npm run api for the Express backend. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and optionally JIRA credentials in .env.
security: Express backend proxies Claude API key -- never exposed to the client
Vite proxy handles JIRA Basic Auth credentials server-side
CORS enabled on the Express server for controlled access
No persistent data storage -- diagrams are ephemeral until exported
performance: Vite ES module bundling with hot module replacement for fast dev cycles
Dagre auto-layout computes node positions in milliseconds
React Flow's optimized canvas handles hundreds of nodes smoothly
html-to-image export renders client-side without server round-trips

kactus-qa.about: QA testing dashboard for the Kactus platform -- automated test execution, reporting, and regression tracking.
features: Automated QA test execution and result tracking
Regression detection across test runs
Visual test reporting with pass/fail dashboards
Integration with CI/CD pipeline for continuous testing
Historical test data for trend analysis
kactus-qa.architect: Web-based QA dashboard for managing and visualizing test results from the Kactus platform. Integrates with existing CI/CD infrastructure for automated test execution and reporting.
kactus-qa.deploy: Clone the repo, install dependencies, and start the dev server. Connects to the Kactus platform API for test data.
security: Scoped to internal QA use only -- no public-facing endpoints
Read-only access to test results by default
No credentials stored client-side
Local network access only
performance: Lightweight dashboard focused on data visualization
Paginated test result loading for large test suites
Client-side filtering avoids repeated API calls
Minimal dependency footprint

local-apps.about: A self-healing app monitor that tracks all your local apps, auto-restarts crashes, runs nightly tests, and deploys AI agents to fix failures.
features: Live dashboard with status, hostnames, LAN/Tailscale/Vercel links for all apps
Auto-restart crashed apps every 30s via launchctl + AI agents fix stubborn failures
Nightly test suite runs 858 tests across 11 repos with auto-fix agents
Screenshot bot captures desktop + mobile with device frames, plus 30s GIF recordings
Multi-machine hub/agent architecture with Tailscale and Cloudflare remote access
local-apps.architect: Express 4 server with SQLite (better-sqlite3) for app registry and state. Hub/agent architecture -- hub orchestrates bots and monitoring, agents report status. Playwright captures screenshots and GIF recordings. Sharp processes images and generates favicons. Compression middleware for responses.
local-apps.deploy: Run npm install && npm start to launch on port 9876. Add apps via POST to /api/apps. Machine role configurable via MACHINE_ROLE env var or machine-role.json (hub or agent).
security: Caddy validates config before reloading -- never serves with broken configuration
Channel-based WebSocket validation with regex patterns
Hub/agent role separation limits what each machine can do
Auto security scan via AI agents flags vulnerabilities
performance: Compression middleware (gzip) on all Express responses
Static asset caching with 1-hour Cache-Control headers, busted by ?v= timestamp
SQLite for fast local reads -- no external database server needed
30s health check interval with quick-fix-first approach before deploying AI agents

clip.about: Tiny clipboard sync daemon -- seamlessly share your clipboard between two Macs over LAN in real time.
features: Real-time clipboard sync between two machines via WebSocket
SHA-256 hash comparison polls every 400ms for instant detection
SQLite-backed clipboard history with deduplication
Echo prevention with 2-second cooldown after receiving
launchd service for always-on background operation
clip.architect: Node.js Express server with WebSocket (ws library) for peer-to-peer clipboard sync. Polls pbpaste every 400ms and compares SHA-256 hashes to detect changes. SQLite stores clipboard history with dedup logic. launchd plist for macOS service management.
clip.deploy: Run CLIP_NAME=M4 CLIP_PEER=<peer-ip>:4545 CLIP_TOKEN=<secret> node src/server.js on each machine. Install as a persistent launchd service.
security: Shared token (CLIP_TOKEN) authenticates peer connections
Never stores clipboard content in plaintext -- only SHA-256 hashes for comparison
LAN-only peer connections -- no cloud relay
Echo prevention stops infinite sync loops between machines
performance: SHA-256 hash comparison avoids transferring unchanged clipboard content
400ms polling interval balances responsiveness with CPU efficiency
In-memory dedup with periodic SQLite flush minimizes disk I/O
WebSocket persistent connection eliminates HTTP handshake overhead per sync

cl-poster.about: Craigslist post manager with 100 templates, weekly auto-rotation, Playwright automation, and 48-hour cooldown enforcement.
features: 100 post templates with 10/week auto-rotation to avoid duplicate detection
Playwright CLI for semi-automated Craigslist posting
48-hour cooldown enforcement per category
Title and description rotation for variation
Post history tracking with status dashboard
cl-poster.architect: Next.js 16 with React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS 4. SQLite (better-sqlite3) stores templates, rotation state, and posting history. Playwright automates browser-based posting flows via a CLI. Husky for pre-commit hooks.
cl-poster.deploy: Run npm install && npx playwright install chromium && npm run dev to start on port 3009. CLI available via npx ts-node cli/post.ts for listing, posting, rotating, and checking cooldowns.
security: Security headers: X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
Local-only SQLite storage -- no cloud data exposure
Husky pre-commit hooks enforce code quality gates
No external API keys or credentials stored in the app
performance: SQLite for instant local reads -- no network database latency
Tailwind CSS 4 with automatic tree-shaking for minimal stylesheet
Playwright runs headless Chromium for fast automated posting
Next.js 16 with Turbopack for fast dev builds
